title: The $0.01 Trick Stores Use to Empty Your Wallet
hook: That $9.99 price tag isn't saving you a penny — it's a tested psychological weapon costing you $1,400 a year.
angle: Charm pricing (left-digit anchoring) is the most pervasive retail manipulation — MIT proved a $39 dress outsells a $34 dress by 24% simply because of the nine
audience: Anyone who shops at grocery stores, clothing retailers, or online — literally everyone
key_numbers: 24% sales boost from charm pricing in MIT studies
60% of all retail prices end in 9
$1,400/year average overspend triggered by charm pricing
